function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'rand' to reach function 'main'?
Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'rand'?
loop_reachability: Is it possible that the loop with condition 'i < 10' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'i < 10' always executed?
Can the loop with condition 'i <9' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'i <9' always executed?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 7:  <statement> int t; </statement> is reached, the statement line 9:  <statement> for(i =0; i < 10; i++) </statement> has already been reached?
Does the statement line 14:  <statement> printf("\n排序后的数组:\n"); </statement> dominate line 23:  <statement> a[j]=t; </statement>?
data_dependency: Does variable a at line 5: <statement>   int a[10]; </statement> determine the value stored in variable t at line 21: <statement> 	      t = a[i]; </statement>?
Does the value of variable j at line 17: <statement>       for(j = i +1; j<10; j++) </statement> depend on the value of variable t at line 7: <statement>   int t; </statement>?
liveness: Is variable 'i' live at the end of function 'main'?
Does variable 'j' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Can the program actually reach the statement: “int t;”?
